name: Vlarimil Orius
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears upper-class clothing consisting of a green brocade doublet, a green silk garment, a piece of jewelry, and green velvet shoes - elegant formal attire appropriate for a noble guest at the Countess's dinner party. This refined appearance is maintained until the effects of Stark Reality take hold.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: refined, dignified, vulnerable, embarrassed, victimized
Role: Vlarimil Orius is a noble and guest of Countess Alessia Caro at Castle Leyawiin. He is an unwitting participant in Sanguine's prank during what the Daedric Prince describes as "another excruciating dinner party" at the castle. He becomes one of the victims of the Stark Reality spell, which strips guests of their clothing and dignity.
Location: Castle Leyawiin's county hall, where he appears specifically during Sanguine's quest. He can be found eating there around the clock during the dinner party.
The Dinner Party: Vlarimil attends what Sanguine characterizes as an "excruciating" formal dinner at Castle Leyawiin - presumably a tedious, overly formal affair of the sort nobility must endure. He arrives dressed in his finest green velvet and brocade, expecting a dignified evening of refined company and polite conversation.
Victim of Stark Reality: When the Stark Reality spell is successfully cast, Vlarimil becomes one of its victims. The spell strips him of his clothing, leaving him naked. He initially runs around panicking - a humiliating response for a dignified noble suddenly exposed before the Countess and fellow guests. After this brief period of distressed running, he somehow resumes his meal completely naked, as if trying to maintain some semblance of normalcy despite the absurd situation. This could represent shock, denial, or simply not knowing what else to do in such an unprecedented circumstance.
Personality - The Dignified Victim: Vlarimil represents the collision between noble dignity and Daedric chaos. He arrives as a proper noble guest, dressed impeccably, ready to fulfill his social obligations at yet another formal dinner. When Sanguine's prank strips away both his clothing and his dignity, his initial panic is understandable - this is a nightmare scenario for someone of his station, whose reputation and standing depend on maintaining propriety. That he eventually returns to eating despite being naked suggests either profound shock (going through the motions as his mind tries to process what happened), stubborn determination to maintain some form of normalcy, or perhaps the realization that there's nothing to be done but endure the situation. He's a victim of circumstances beyond his control, caught up in a Daedric Prince's idea of entertainment at a dinner party he probably didn't want to attend in the first place.
Temporary Existence: Vlarimil only exists for the duration of Sanguine's quest. Once the player returns to Sanguine's Shrine or serves time in jail for the disruption, he is removed from the game entirely - as if the entire dinner party and its guests were conjured specifically for the prank and then dismissed when no longer needed for the Daedric Prince's amusement.
author: Bethesda
